evidence: risk_model.py uses a consistent try/except ValueError pattern for ROC AUC across all four training functions (lines 138-142, 210-214, 268-271, 403-406, 487-491), logging a warning and falling back to NaN. This is well-designed and predictable.

Validation functions in gnn_dataset.py (lines 103-106, 130-134) and risk_model.py (lines 175-180, 368-373, 452-458) consistently raise ValueError/FileNotFoundError with descriptive messages for invalid inputs.

Scripts have inconsistent error handling discipline: train_design_risk_model.py checks for file existence before calling library functions (line 33-37), but export_design_dataset.py and export_failure_dataset.py perform no error handling around generate_tier1_temporal_dataset() or CSV writing operations.

hypatia_adapter.py line 975 has a bare 'except Exception: pass' that silently swallows errors during temp directory cleanup, though this is in a __del__-adjacent cleanup method where swallowing is defensible.

The load_*_dataset functions in risk_model.py (load_design_dataset, load_failure_dataset, load_tier1_dataset, load_tier1_v1_dataset) all call pd.read_csv() without try/except, letting pandas KeyError or FileNotFoundError propagate raw with no added context. Some callers (train_design_risk_model.py) add their own FileNotFoundError check, creating split error responsibility.

SchemaValidationError is the only custom exception in the codebase (monte_carlo.py line 347), while structurally similar validation errors in risk_model.py and gnn_dataset.py use plain ValueError, making it harder to distinguish schema failures from other value errors at catch boundaries.

issues.summary: load_*_dataset functions let pandas errors propagate without added context or wrapping
evidence: load_design_dataset (line 100), load_failure_dataset (line 228), load_tier1_dataset (line 156), and load_tier1_v1_dataset (line 308) all call pd.read_csv() and then index into df[COLUMNS] without try/except. A missing column raises a raw pandas KeyError with no information about which dataset or expected schema. Meanwhile, the caller train_design_risk_model.py adds a FileNotFoundError check (line 33), splitting the error-handling responsibility inconsistently between library and script layers.
issues.suggestion: Wrap pd.read_csv() and column selection in each load_*_dataset function with try/except that catches FileNotFoundError and KeyError, re-raising as a domain-specific error (e.g., DatasetLoadError) with context about which file and which columns were expected. This centralizes error handling in the library rather than forcing callers to guard independently.

issues.summary: Export scripts lack error handling around dataset generation and CSV I/O operations
evidence: export_design_dataset.py calls generate_tier1_temporal_dataset(cfg) at line 147 and write_tier1_dataset_csv() at line 156 with no try/except. If the Hypatia simulation fails for any run (e.g., invalid constellation parameters), the entire script crashes with an unhandled exception. Similarly, export_failure_dataset.py (line 41, 49) and failure_sweep.py (line 38) have no error handling. By contrast, train_design_risk_model.py properly checks for file existence and would benefit from similar treatment in sibling scripts.
issues.suggestion: Add top-level try/except in each script's main() function to catch expected failures (ValueError from invalid parameters, FileNotFoundError, SchemaValidationError) with user-friendly error messages and non-zero exit codes, matching the pattern already established in train_design_risk_model.py and train_gnn_model.py.

issues.summary: SchemaValidationError used in monte_carlo.py but plain ValueError used for equivalent validation elsewhere
evidence: monte_carlo.py defines SchemaValidationError(ValueError) at line 347 for dataset schema validation (missing columns, invalid ranges). However, gnn_dataset.py uses plain ValueError for structurally identical column-missing checks (line 132), and risk_model.py uses plain ValueError for single-class dataset validation (lines 176, 369, 453). A caller trying to catch schema-specific failures vs. other value errors cannot distinguish them in the risk_model/gnn_dataset code paths.
issues.suggestion: Either adopt SchemaValidationError consistently across all dataset loading/validation code (risk_model.py column checks, gnn_dataset.py column checks), or remove the custom exception and use plain ValueError everywhere with consistent message formatting. The former is preferable since it enables callers to distinguish schema failures from other validation errors.

root_causes: Error handling was added organically as each module was written, without a project-wide error strategy. The core training functions in risk_model.py share a consistent pattern (ROC AUC try/except), but the boundary between library and script error responsibility was never formalized.
The load_*_dataset functions treat CSV loading as infallible, pushing error handling responsibility to callers inconsistently.
likely_symptoms: script_missing_error_handling is a symptom of the broader missing_io_boundary_handling root cause
possible_false_positives: mixed_validation_exception_types may be intentional if SchemaValidationError was designed only for the monte_carlo schema contract and plain ValueError is preferred elsewhere
